scope: Home, Library, and Reader/Audiobook frontend UX only

current_live_ux_gaps.gap: Source already uses the hybrid editorial hero and three curated action cards, but CTAs and nav labels need final Figma naming and analytics alignment.
Library still has Dracula-heavy hero CTAs and category-only filtering instead of a premium recognition-first catalog with language, availability, sort, and view controls.
Reader has a strong secure shell, but audio microcopy can imply generic synced audio and settings lack explicit margin, focus, reduced-motion, and highlight controls.

target_figma_ux_architecture.home: Compact header, calm editorial hero, three curated action cards, release-gated approved-audio area, Bengali and English shelves below fold.
target_figma_ux_architecture.library: Premium catalog with compact hero, search, language chips, availability chips, sort selector, view mode selector, graphical cards, and intentional reader-only states.
target_figma_ux_architecture.reader: Reading-first shell with toolbar, calm page surface, mobile-safe settings sheet, optional approved-audiobook mini player, and paragraph/stanza sync wording when appropriate.

cover_system_requirements: No public cover may be typographic-only.
Use the existing graphical cover resolver and audit script.
Do not add paid image-generation or heavy client-side image libraries.
Preserve alt text, aspect ratios, lazy loading, and no cover clipping.
release_gate_risks: Reader controls must remain hidden unless canExposeAudiobookControls returns true.
A Ghost Story must not be probed or shown as audio by default.
Paragraph/stanza sync must not be labeled as word-level sync.
Browser/system speech fallback must not be presented as an audiobook.
performance_risks: Extra catalog controls must not add heavy dependencies.
Below-fold shelves must remain lazy/idle friendly.
No new large raster cover assets should be introduced.
Typography and layout changes must preserve CLS and mobile overflow constraints.
implementation_plan: Align header nav and homepage CTA labels to final Figma language.
Upgrade Library to include language, availability, sort, and view controls with calm compact styling.
Refine BookCard availability badges and listen CTA gating.
Add Reader settings for margin, focus, reduced motion, and highlight intensity; clarify approved-audio and unavailable-audio microcopy.
Add CSS design tokens matching the fixed palette and calm typography scale.
Create Figma-ready spec and scorecard artifacts.
rollback_plan: Revert only frontend UX source files changed in this pass.
Keep audioReleaseSafety and backend/publishing logic untouched unless a release-gate regression is found.
Do not revert unrelated dirty worktree changes.
